marketState: neutral, mildly bearish bias - price sits right at fair value with a bearish trend backdrop and a bearish storage surprise, but hot near-term weather demand supports tactical bounce risk
summary: Front-month NG trades at $2.948, essentially in line with fair value across all horizons (spreads of $0.03 or less against fair values of $2.917-$3.015, band roughly $2.42-$3.52), so there is no valuation mispricing to exploit right now. Seasonality data (vs-median, probability-higher, typical 4-week move, reliability) is entirely missing from this snapshot, removing the top-priority input and forcing lower conviction on the longer-horizon view. Storage posted a much larger build than forecast (+61 Bcf actual vs -11 Bcf expected, a +72 Bcf bearish surprise) and the surplus vs the 5-year average widened to +184 Bcf, a bearish supply-side signal. Daily and weekly technicals show a bearish trend structure (50<200 EMA cross, negative MACD, price below the lower daily Bollinger band) with the daily/4h RSI oversold, while managed-money COT positioning is net short (-60,295 contracts) but only at the 35th percentile, i.e. not a crowded/extreme position. Near-term (1-7D) weather demand is running +17.9% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range, arguing for near-term price support even as demand normalizes further out (8-16D at -2% vs 5y) and the 24h demand trend is already softening.

longTerm.rationale: A much larger-than-forecast storage build (+72 Bcf surprise) and a growing +184 Bcf surplus vs the 5-year average point to a well-supplied market into the fall, reinforced by a bearish weekly/daily trend structure (50<200 EMA cross, negative MACD) and modestly net-short but non-extreme COT positioning that leaves room for further length to be added on the short side. Fair value itself is roughly flat/rising only slightly ($2.92 now to $3.02 by +21D) so there is no fundamental floor being signaled beneath current price.
longTerm.invalidation: A run of larger-than-expected storage draws/smaller builds that closes the 5y surplus, or a sustained rally back above the fair-value band midpoint with 8-16D weather demand climbing back above 5y normal.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D weather demand is running +17.9% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range and the front contract is trading at a slight premium/backwardation to Sep (-$0.048 spread), both consistent with near-term tightness, while 4h/1d RSI are oversold and daily price sits below the lower Bollinger band, setting up a mean-reversion bounce toward fair value ($2.92-$2.95).
shortTerm.invalidation: 8-16D demand fails to hold near normal and rolls over further below 5y average, or price breaks and holds below $2.78, confirming the bearish 1h-1w trend structure is back in control.

keyDrivers: Storage: +72 Bcf bearish surprise build, 5y surplus widened to +184 Bcf
Price is essentially at fair value on all horizons (now/+7D/+14D/+21D spreads all <=$0.07) - no mispricing edge
Daily/weekly technicals bearish (death-cross EMA, negative MACD) but daily/4h RSI oversold, hinting at a bounce
Near-term weather demand +17.9% vs 5y (top of range) fading toward normal by 8-16D (-2% vs 5y)
COT managed money net short (-60,295) but only 35th percentile - not a crowded/extreme position
risks: Seasonality inputs (vsMedian, probHigher, typicalMove4w, reliability) are all null/missing in this snapshot - a top-priority input is unavailable, lowering confidence in both trade ideas
Weather demand can shift quickly (24h change already -11.1), which could flip the near-term bullish driver behind the short-term long
Several underlying data feeds (Yahoo/CORS proxies) failed in this snapshot - confirm live price and curve data before acting on these levels
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
